behavior: Usually forages high in the canopy, sitting quietly before making short aerial sallies for insects or gleaning along branches.
field_marks: Chunky, medium-sized songbird with a large head and thick, blunt-tipped bill.
Adult male is uniformly bright rosy red, including wings and tail.
Female and immature male are yellow to yellow-green, often yellower below and greener on back and wings.
Bill is conspicuously pale or horn-colored; eye is dark.
Wings are plain and rounded, without bold wingbars.
Tail is medium-long and broad, typically rounded to square-tipped.
habitat: Open deciduous and mixed woodlands, forest gaps and edges, and wooded streamside groves; also uses open and second-growth wooded habitats.

sources.title: Summer Tanager Identification — All About Birds
